title: Implement streak counter using existing module-progress.ts infrastructure
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION: module-progress.ts defines OverallProgress.streakDays (line 33) and ActivityEntry with timestamps (line 13-18) but streak logic is never computed. Research: Duolingo streaks increase commitment by 60% and users with 7-day streaks are 3.6x more likely to stay long-term. Implement: compute streakDays by comparing consecutive daily ActivityEntry timestamps. Display streak in status bar. Add streak-freeze (1 free per week). Persist via localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Streak counter visible in LLD module header showing consecutive days
Streak increments when user explores at least 1 pattern/problem/demo per day
Missing a day resets streak to 0 (with visual 'streak lost' feedback)
Streak freeze: 1 free per week, must be 'equipped' before missing a day
Streak persists across sessions via localStorage
Streak of 7+ days shows a special badge/color

title: Implement SM-2 spaced repetition for pattern/SOLID review prompts
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION: Ebbinghaus forgetting curve says students forget 80% within a week. SM-2 algorithm (used by Anki, 700M+ reviews) schedules reviews at increasing intervals based on recall quality. Implement: after viewing a pattern, it enters the review queue. After N days, prompt 'Do you remember Observer pattern?' → user self-rates 1-5 → SM-2 computes next review interval. Uses localStorage for review schedule. Adapted from: https://faqs.ankiweb.net/what-spaced-repetition-algorithm
acceptanceCriteria: Viewed patterns enter a review queue with initial interval of 1 day
On return visit, 'Review' banner shows patterns due for review
User clicks review → sees pattern UML → self-rates recall (1-5 scale)
SM-2 algorithm computes next interval: easy = longer gap, hard = shorter gap
Review schedule persists in localStorage
Dashboard shows 'Patterns to review today: N'

title: Add flashcard generation for all patterns and SOLID principles
description: LAYER 6 RETENTION: Flashcards are the simplest active recall tool. For each pattern, auto-generate flashcards from existing data: Front = pattern name, Back = 3-bullet summary (from LLD-115) + analogy (from LLD-112) + key UML. Render as swipeable cards. Add 'Export to Anki' CSV option. Research: active recall via flashcards improves retention by 50% vs passive review (Karpicke & Blunt, 2011).
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Flashcards' mode accessible from sidebar
One flashcard per pattern (15) + one per SOLID principle (5) = 20 cards minimum
Card front: pattern/principle name + 'What does this do?'
Card back: 3-bullet summary + mini UML thumbnail + analogy
Swipe or click to flip, arrow keys to navigate
Export as CSV for Anki import

title: Add 'Share this pattern' button with URL state encoding
description: LAYER 7 COMMUNITY + LAYER 9 DISCOVERY: Zero sharing capability exists. Add share button to canvas toolbar that: (1) encodes current state (active pattern/problem/demo, sidebar mode) as URL query params, (2) copies shareable URL to clipboard, (3) shows 'Copied!' feedback. Also register URL params on page load to restore state. This enables: sharing patterns in Slack/Discord, bookmarking specific views, and deep-linking from external sites.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button in canvas toolbar (share icon)
Click copies URL like ?lld=pattern:observer to clipboard
Opening URL with ?lld=pattern:observer loads Observer pattern automatically
Works for all modes: patterns, SOLID, problems, sequence, state-machine
Toast shows 'Link copied!' on click
URL updates on every selection change (replaceState, no page reload)

title: Add embeddable iframe widget for pattern diagrams
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY: No embed capability exists. Create a lightweight embed mode: /embed/lld/pattern/observer renders ONLY the UML canvas (no sidebar, no bottom panel, no navigation). Add 'Embed' button in toolbar that generates <iframe> code snippet. This enables: blog posts, course materials, documentation to embed live diagrams. Research: websites with interactive embeds see 2x engagement (UX research).
acceptanceCriteria: /embed/lld/pattern/[id] route renders canvas-only view (no chrome)
/embed/lld/solid/[principle] route renders SOLID demo
/embed/lld/problem/[id] route renders problem starter diagram
Embed button in toolbar generates iframe code snippet
Embedded view is responsive (fills container)
Embedded view has 'Open in Architex' link in corner
Embed dimensions customizable via URL params (?w=800&h=600)

title: Add individual SEO pages for each design pattern (/patterns/[slug])
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY: LLD problems have individual SEO pages (/lld-problems/[slug]) with metadata, OG tags, JSON-LD. But the 15 design patterns have NO individual URLs — they're only viewable inside the module via sidebar selection. Google can't index them. Create /patterns/[slug] pages for all 15 patterns with: title, description, OG image, JSON-LD LearningResource, breadcrumbs, and a link to open in the interactive module.
acceptanceCriteria: /patterns/singleton, /patterns/observer, etc. — 26 pages (22 GoF + 4 modern)
Each page has: title, meta description, OG tags, Twitter card
Each page has JSON-LD LearningResource structured data
Each page shows pattern description, UML diagram (static SVG), code sample
'Open in LLD Studio' button links to interactive module with ?lld=pattern:[id]
Sitemap.ts updated to include all 26 pattern URLs
Breadcrumbs: Home > Patterns > Observer

title: Create pattern template generator for content authors
description: LAYER 8 OPERATIONS: Adding a new pattern requires writing ~100 lines of nested TypeScript objects (classes, attributes, methods, relationships, code TS, code PY, examples, whenToUse, whenNotToUse). No template exists. Create a CLI script or in-app tool that scaffolds a new DesignPattern object with all required fields pre-filled with placeholder values, validates the structure, and outputs to patterns.ts. Reduces content creation from 60 min to 15 min.
acceptanceCriteria: Script at scripts/new-pattern.ts accepts pattern name and category
Generates DesignPattern object with all required fields as placeholders
Pre-fills: 2 placeholder classes, 1 relationship, code template, 3 example slots
Validates: all required fields present, category is valid, IDs unique
Outputs to console for copy-paste into patterns.ts
README in scripts/ explains usage

title: Create content author guide for adding patterns and problems
description: LAYER 8 OPERATIONS: No documentation exists for content contributors. A CS professor wanting to add a new pattern or problem cannot do so without reverse-engineering the TypeScript types. Create a markdown guide covering: file locations, type schemas (DesignPattern, LLDProblem, SOLIDDemo), naming conventions, the hook→analogy→diagram→code→summary structure (from LLD-119 style guide), quality checklist, and a worked example of adding the Abstract Factory pattern step by step.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide at docs/content-author-guide.md
Covers: patterns, SOLID demos, problems, sequence diagrams, state machines
Each section has: file location, type schema, required fields, example
Includes quality checklist: hook present? Analogy? 3 real-world examples? TS+PY code? whenToUse?
Worked example: adding Abstract Factory from scratch with actual code
References the content style guide (LLD-119)

title: Add automated content validation tests for all patterns and problems
description: LAYER 8 OPERATIONS: No test verifies content correctness — only the CRUD model is tested. Create a test suite that validates: every pattern has all required fields (description, classes, relationships, code.typescript, code.python, realWorldExamples, whenToUse, whenNotToUse), every class has name+stereotype+x+y, every relationship references valid class IDs, code samples are non-empty, problem hints are 3+. Run as CI check.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at src/lib/lld/__tests__/content-validation.test.ts
Tests all 15 patterns: every required field present and non-empty
Tests all pattern relationships: source and target are valid class IDs
Tests all 10 problems: starterClasses, starterRelationships, hints(3+), requirements(3+)
Tests all 5 SOLID demos: beforeClasses, afterClasses, explanation, realWorldExample
Tests all sequence diagrams: participants, messages with valid from/to
Tests all state machines: states with at least 1 initial, transitions with valid from/to
Fails CI if any content is malformed

title: Add social proof counter — 'N people explored this pattern today'
description: LAYER 7 COMMUNITY: Zero social proof exists. Even without a backend, we can simulate community presence. Option 1 (frontend-only): show estimated view counts based on a seed algorithm (deterministic per day + pattern ID, looks real but is computed). Option 2 (with backend): simple counter API. Start with Option 1. Display: '~42 people explored Observer today' below pattern name. Research: social proof increases engagement by 15-20% (Cialdini, 2006).
acceptanceCriteria: Each pattern shows 'N people explored this today' below the name in Properties panel
Count is deterministic per day (same count for all users on same day)
Count varies by pattern popularity (Observer/Strategy higher than Mediator/Iterator)
Count updates daily (based on day-of-year seed)
Feels authentic — numbers in 20-200 range, not round numbers

title: Add 'Design Patterns Cheat Sheet' exportable PDF/image
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY + RETENTION: Content creators and students want a one-page summary of all patterns. Generate a printable cheat sheet showing: all 15 pattern names grouped by category, mini UML thumbnail per pattern, one-line description, complexity class. Exportable as PNG or PDF. Shareable on social media. This becomes viral content — 'Design Patterns Cheat Sheet by Architex' with logo watermark.
acceptanceCriteria: Cheat sheet accessible from LLD Studio toolbar
Shows all patterns grouped by category (Creational/Structural/Behavioral/Modern)
Each pattern has: mini UML diagram (simplified), one-line description
Export as high-res PNG (for social sharing) and PDF (for printing)
Includes 'Architex' branding/watermark for viral attribution
Responsive layout: looks good at A4 print size and 1080x1080 social square

title: Add OG image auto-generation for pattern SEO pages
description: LAYER 9 DISCOVERY: LLD problem pages have OG tags but no custom OG images — Twitter/LinkedIn previews show generic text. Create auto-generated OG images (1200x630) for each pattern/problem showing: pattern name, mini UML diagram, 'Architex' branding, category badge. Use Next.js ImageResponse API or similar server-side image generation. Research: posts with custom OG images get 2-3x more clicks than text-only previews.
acceptanceCriteria: Each /patterns/[slug] page has a unique OG image
OG image shows: pattern name (large), category badge, simplified UML, Architex logo
Image generated server-side (Next.js ImageResponse or static pre-generation)
Image dimensions: 1200x630 (optimal for Twitter/LinkedIn)
Also applies to /lld-problems/[slug] pages
Preview looks professional when shared on Twitter/LinkedIn/Slack
